question:
Will Bitcoin reach $115,000 in November?

Description: This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT during the month specified in the title (from 00:00 AM ET on the first day to 11:59 PM ET on the last), has a final High price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT High prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance BTC/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

evidences.title: Bitcoin (BTC) Outlook By End of 2025 (Top Forecasters) - Crypto.com
evidences.content: Bernstein projects Bitcoin could reach around $200,000 by the end of 2025, as part of a broader outlook that sees the current bull market potentially extending through 2027. VanEck maintains a $180,000 Bitcoin price target for the end of 2025, citing strong institutional demand and a maturing ETF market. ## Crypto-native bulls: Tom Lee, Michael Saylor, Cathie Wood and more While banks have nudged expectations lower, several well-known crypto advocates are still holding the line on far more aggressive targets. ### Tom Lee (Fundstrat): $200K to $250K Fundstrat’s Tom Lee still argues that Bitcoin could end 2025 in a $150,000 to $200,000 range, describing a $200,000 year-end target as achievable if policy, ETF flows and market sentiment remain supportive. His thesis leans on: [...] ### Standard Chartered: From $200K down to $100K Standard Chartered has been one of the most active large banks in publishing explicit Bitcoin targets. Earlier in 2025 it floated a path to $135,000 by Q3 and $200,000 by year-end, driven by ETF inflows and corporate treasury adoption. After the October drawdown and slowing ETF flows, the bank cut its end-2025 forecast to $100,000, saying the cycle is likel...

evidences.title: 8 Predictions for Bitcoin Price in 2025: What Banks, Hedge Funds, and Experts Say
evidences.content: ### Bitfinex: $200,000 Cryptocurrency exchange Bitfinex released a report on December 10, stating that Bitcoin could reach $200,000 by mid-2025. The analysis noted that the minimum mid-cycle price could be around $145,000, with a bullish scenario pushing the price to $200,000. Bitfinex researchers draw parallels with the 2021 cycle when Bitcoin rose about 40% from its moving average, indicating the possibility of an even higher peak. However, they note that "the trend of diminishing returns observed in recent cycles" suggests a more moderate rally into the $160,000–$200,000 range by mid-2025. ### Peter Brandt: $125,000–$150,000 [...] While their baseline forecast suggests Bitcoin will reach a market capitalization of around $4.5 trillion—about 25% of gold's value—this note implies even higher price potential if the U.S. government under Donald Trump adopts BTC as a strategic reserve asset. ### Standard Chartered: $250,000 Standard Chartered, after accurately predicting Bitcoin's price increase to $100,000 by 2024, is now witnessing a sustained rally that could push prices to $200,000 or higher by the end of 2025—potentially even up to $250,000 if certain conditions are met. [......

evidences.content: Watch Wednesdays, 3 PM ET Crypto World # Bitcoin could hit $150,000 by 2025, predicts Bernstein Melina Khan WATCH LIVE Key Points Bernstein said Tuesday it expects the price of bitcoin could rise to $150,000 by mid-2025. The estimate is nearly five times the current price of $35,000. Last week, the price of bitcoin reached its highest level since May 2022, as investors remain hopeful for a green light on a bitcoin exchange-traded fund. evidences.title: Cryptocurrency Market & Bitcoin Predictions for 2025 - Galaxy
evidences.content: ## Bitcoin Predictions for 2025 Bitcoin will cross $150k in H1 and test or best $185k in Q4 2025. A combination of institutional, corporate, and nation-state adoption will propel Bitcoin to new heights in 2025. Throughout its existence, Bitcoin has appreciated faster than all other asset classes, particularly the S&P 500 and gold, and that trend will continue in 2025. Bitcoin will also reach 20% of Gold’s market cap. -Alex Thorn Note: A prior version of the graphic above misstated some CAGR numbers for Gold and S&P 500. It has been updated with the correct numbers.

"q":"3715253.19494",// Quote asset volume "V":"184.769",// Taker buy volume "Q":"3472925.84746",// Taker buy quote asset volume "B": "0"// Ignore}}< pair> _< contractType> @continuousKline_< interval> ``` [...] Supported kline intervals: : seconds: 1s minutes: 1m, 3m, 5m, 15m, 30m hours: 1h, 2h, 4h, 6h, 8h, 12h days: 1d, 3d weeks: 1w months: 1M Notes: : If startTime/endTime not specified, returns most recent klines Supported timeZone values: : + Hours and minutes (e.g. “-1:00”, “05:45”) + Only hours (e.g. “0”, “8”, “4”) + Accepted range is strictly [-12:00 to +14:00] inclusive If timeZone provided, kline intervals are interpreted in that timezone instead of...

evidences.title: Getting Historic Crypto Prices with Binance API
evidences.content: ## Understanding the Binance API We will use Binance’s Kline/Candlestick Data API endpoint. It takes 5 parameters and gives 12 return figures including OHLCV explained below. ### Input Parameters symbol — BNBUSDT interval —“1m” representing 1 minute startTime —UTC in ms. Will be set dynamically (ex. 1636710431000) endTime — Start from midnight yesterday and go back by 13 days each limit — 1000 for maximum efficiency Press enter or click to view image in full size Image 5 Screenshot of sample API response from Binance ### Output Returns (for BNBUSDT symbol by minute) [...] Sitemap Open in app Sign up Sign in with the Binance API. ### Why Binance? evidences.title: Bitcoin Price - History, Chart, 5 Years, 10 Years, Halving
evidences.content: ## What is Bitcoin's all-time high price record? November 2021 saw Bitcoin's all-time high price record, around $69,000. This milestone captures the rising institutional interest in blockchain technology as well as their acceptance. But Bitcoin's volatility means its price might vary greatly, hence investors have to exercise great caution. The price swings of Bitcoin rely on several elements, including market mood, laws, and more general state of the economy. Investing in Bitcoin presents hazards even if it presents chances. Before investing, one should investigate carefully and get financial guidance. [...] Combining speculative bubbles, outside pressure, and legislative changes usually produces price crashes. Knowing these elements helps one negotiate the erratic market of Bitcoin. ## What are the key milestones in Bitcoin's price history? Several turning points in Bitcoin's price history have molded its market path. When Bitcoin initially crossed $1,000 in 2013, investors became rather excited. Driven by speculative excitement, Bitcoin hit almost $20,000 in 2017, marking still another important turning point. Driven by economic instability and inflation worries, institutional...

evidences.title: Statistics on How Bitcoin Moves - average rally and pullback ...
evidences.content: how bitcoin acts when retesting all-time highs The choppy action around the old high persisted for 8 months, until the price made a strong move into new high territory in November 2024 (not shown). How bitcoin reacts near old highs, 2024 scenario After testing an old all-time high, following a 70% decline, the price has generally chopped around and then proceeded higher after the chop. That may not always happen, but that is what the historical examples have done so far. Prices have ultimately continued higher after the volatility. 2017 was a notable exception, but it was also not preceded by a major bear market. Recoveries following severe bear markets (70%+ declines) tend to be very strong, pushing the price well above prior all-time highs as the uptrend unfolds. [...] There was a non-severe bear market that followed, declining 55%. Bitcoin reclaimed the prior high, setting a slightly higher all-time high in late 2021. This too saw lots of whipsaws but isn’t included in our analysis because the test of the all-time high wasn’t preceded by a deep and drawn-out severe bear market (70%+ decline). The all-time high in late 2021 at $69,000 stood until 2024. The level was marginally...

evidences.title: Bitcoin's price history (2009-2025) – key market events, data charts ...
evidences.content: ### 2012–2013: Building momentum After the 2011 crash, Bitcoin began climbing again. Prices and trading volume slowly picked up as more people joined the network and exchanges became easier to use. Bitcoin was no longer just for tech-savvy users as it started to reach a broader audience. By 2013, momentum had clearly shifted. Bitcoin passed $100 in April, then hit $200 in October6. In November, it reached $1,000 on Mt. Gox for the first time. Bitcoin started getting real attention. News sites covered it more often, stores and services began accepting it, and investors started treating it like more than just a novelty. It had moved beyond the idea of digital money and was emerging as a credible alternative to fiat currency and a new kind of financial asset. [...] Over the years, Bitcoin has faced extreme volatility. It has rallied, like its 8,000% surge in 2011, and plunged, like the 80% drawdown during the 2018 crypto winter. These swings were often aggravated by their speculative nature and sensitivity to macroeconomic forces, including inflation trends and central bank policies. Despite this turbulence, Bitcoin steadily transformed from a fringe internet experiment into a reco...

evidences.title: Is Bitcoin Bottom Near? BTC Approaches 'Death Cross' as Market Tests Key Historical Pattern
evidences.content: In September 2023, bitcoin bottomed near $25,000, in August 2024 during the yen carry trade unwind it found support around $49,000, and then in April 2025, during uncertainty around President Trump’s tariff policy, BTC bottomed below $75,000. In the current setup, bitcoin has fallen to $94,000 and in all four prior instances the market put in its low just before the death cross formed, raising the question of whether the same pattern may be unfolding again. ## Is this time different? This current drawdown is less severe than the April correction, when bitcoin dropped below $75,000 during the tariff related turmoil.
